Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed Precision Quantization: A Training-Free Framework for Enhanced Model Efficiency
Short Hypothesis: Can we leverage curvature information to design a training-free mixed precision quantization framework that maintains high accuracy while improving computational efficiency? This approach could bridge the gap in understanding the impact of quantization on model performance, which existing training-free methods do not fully explore.
Related Work: Previous studies focus on training-free proxies for quantization (e.g., EMQ, DF-MPC) but often overlook the role of curvature in model sensitivity to quantization. Unlike these existing frameworks, this proposal directly incorporates curvature information to improve the robustness of quantized models. This novel approach is not merely a variation of existing methods but offers a fresh perspective by systematically integrating curvature diagnostics into the quantization process.
Abstract: In this work, we propose a novel framework for mixed precision quantization that integrates curvature-aware diagnostics to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of deep learning models. Traditional approaches to mixed precision quantization often rely on empirical heuristics or training-based methods, leading to suboptimal performance in resource-constrained environments. Our proposed method leverages curvature information to assess the sensitivity of model layers to quantization errors, enabling a more informed allocation of bit-widths across layers without the need for retraining. We will develop a curvature-aware routing protocol that quantifies the impact of quantization on model performance, facilitating precision tiering that minimizes computational overhead while ensuring model integrity. Through rigorous experiments, we aim to demonstrate that our framework not only achieves superior performance compared to existing training-free methods but also provides clearer insights into the relationship between model architecture, curvature, and quantization effects.
Experiments: 1. Implement the curvature-aware quantization framework on standard architectures such as ResNet and MobileNet, focusing on layer-wise bit-width allocation based on curvature diagnostics.
2. Compare performance against existing training-free methods like EMQ and DF-MPC using standard datasets such as ImageNet.
3. Evaluate the impact of varying curvature thresholds on model accuracy and computational efficiency, measuring metrics such as accuracy drops and speedup ratios.
4. Conduct ablation studies to isolate the contributions of curvature diagnostics from other factors in the quantization process.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the computational overhead of calculating curvature metrics and the challenge of generalizing findings across different model architectures. Limitations may arise from the inherent complexity of curvature analysis in high-dimensional spaces, which could affect the scalability of the proposed method.
